title: Rinorea calophylla
intro: Rinorea calophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae, commonly known as the Calophylla vine or Yellow Madder. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea calophylla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional medicine, Rinorea calophylla is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, capsules, and ointments to harness its health benefits.
